mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market, which supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) is consistently negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, it signals persistent inflation, forcing the central bank to tighten monetary policy more aggressively than anticipated, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Strong momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates healthy consumer spending, a primary driver of GDP growth and corporate revenue, leading to positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: A strategy going long S&P 500 when the 3-month change in RSAFS is positive and short when negative fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a deceleration in payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a rapid deterioration in the labor market, a strong leading indicator of recession and negative for equities.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in over 40% of the periods following the activation of this joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, further increases in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) have a magnified negative impact on equity discount rates and credit conditions, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly changes in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly more negative when FEDFUNDS > 2.0% compared to when it is <= 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive year-over-year growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy, supporting corporate profitability and driving positive equity returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO YoY growth and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a key historical level indicates a severe contraction in a critical, interest-rate sensitive sector, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level falls below 1200 thousands of units
failure_condition: In periods following HOUST dropping below 1200, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an overheating economy, forcing the central bank into a hawkish stance that is detrimental to equity markets.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return when the trigger condition is met is not statistically significantly lower than the average return when it is not met (p > 0.10).
